claims_verified.claim: AC#1: onboard encodes env with trailing newline so appended VAR=value parses independently
claims_verified.evidence: scripts/vps-onboard.sh:202 uses printf '%s\n'; tests/test_vps_onboard.py behavioral+negative-control pass (test_appended_var_parses_independently, test_reproduces_the_original_glue_bug_without_newline)

claims_verified.claim: AC#2: run_lock reclaims a lock whose pid is dead and refuses a live holder
claims_verified.evidence: orchestrator/queue.py:144-166 reclaim path; tests test_run_lock_reclaims_stale_lock_from_dead_holder / test_run_lock_refuses_when_holder_alive / test_pid_alive / test_pid_alive_permission_error_means_alive / test_run_lock_reclaims_corrupt_lock_file pass

claims_verified.claim: Existing per-task exclusivity contract still holds
claims_verified.evidence: tests/test_queue.py test_run_lock_is_exclusive_per_task passes in full run

claims_verified.claim: AC#3: kickoff launches loop unbuffered with heartbeat gate; heartbeat env-gated
claims_verified.evidence: scripts/kickoff.sh:70 `LADDY_LOG_HEARTBEAT=1 nohup "$PY" -u`; orchestrator/artifacts.py:154-165 env-gated stderr print; tests test_kickoff_launches_loop_observably + test_append_log_heartbeat_env_gated pass
orchestrator/loop.py:929 passes round=rp.round, action, outcome; artifacts.py reads fields.get('round'/'action'/'outcome') with graceful default

claims_verified.claim: AC#4: env.vps.example drops the duplicate-variable trap and documents fast vs Docker gate + venv-bootstrapping TEST_COMMANDS
claims_verified.evidence: env.vps.example: AGENT_REPO_URL/AGENT_WORK_ROOT now commented placeholders; TEST_COMMANDS example does `test -d .venv || python3 -m venv`; onboard block at vps-onboard.sh:195-198 is the single active source

claims_verified.claim: AC#5: full suite green, no regression

test_assessment: AC#1 covered by test_onboard_encodes_env_with_trailing_newline (static guard), test_appended_var_parses_independently (behavioral repro of the encode/append/source pipeline), and test_reproduces_the_original_glue_bug_without_newline (negative control proving discrimination). AC#2 covered by test_pid_alive + test_pid_alive_permission_error_means_alive (liveness unit incl. pid<=0, dead reaped pid, EPERM=alive), test_run_lock_reclaims_stale_lock_from_dead_holder, test_run_lock_refuses_when_holder_alive, test_run_lock_reclaims_corrupt_lock_file (malformed-input failure mode), and test_run_lock_concurrent_reclaim_raises_queue_locked (race → clean QueueLocked, not a traceback); the pre-existing exclusivity test still passes. AC#3 covered by test_kickoff_launches_loop_observably (wiring guard for -u + LADDY_LOG_HEARTBEAT) and test_append_log_heartbeat_env_gated (off-by-default quiet, on prints with and without round tag). AC#4 is documentation prose (env.vps.example) — not behaviorally testable, but the underlying trap it warns about is guarded by the AC#1 tests. New functions _pid_alive and the reclaim branch each have failure-mode tests (dead pid, EPERM, corrupt file, concurrent race). The heartbeat path is exercised for both the gated-off and gated-on branches.
residual_risks: Narrow TOCTOU in the reclaim path: two processes racing to reclaim the SAME stale lock could interleave such that the loser's unlink(missing_ok=True) at queue.py:155 deletes the winner's freshly re-created live lock, yielding two loops for one task. The window is a few syscalls wide, requires simultaneous crash-recovery starts on a single-node box, and is a strict improvement over the prior behaviour (permanent deadlock after any crash), so not blocking — but worth noting for future hardening (e.g. atomic rename or post-acquire pid re-check).
PID reuse: a recycled dead pid held by an unrelated process would be read as a live holder and refuse reclaim; documented in the code and no worse than prior behaviour.
